title: cornus obliqua
intro: Cornus obliqua, also known as the Forest Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Cornus obliqua are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. The herbal preparations of Cornus obliqua typically involve decocting the roots or bark to make a tea or infusion, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
